blocks.text: Every webpage — including your Simon Says game — is built with three languages: HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

blocks.alt: Three cards showing HTML structures the page, CSS styles it, and JavaScript makes it interactive
blocks.caption: Three languages, three jobs. Each one does only its part.

blocks.label: HOW THEY CONNECT
blocks.text: JavaScript finds an HTML element, then adds or removes a CSS class. That class change is what makes a square light up — JS doesn't draw anything, it just tells CSS when to apply.

blocks.alt: Diagram showing JS adding a class to an HTML element which triggers a CSS style change
blocks.caption: JS triggers the change. CSS does the actual styling.

blocks.label: KEY RULE
blocks.text: JS never writes colours directly. It only adds and removes class names. CSS is always in charge of what things look like.
